title: opuntia infesta
intro: Opuntia infesta, also known as the teddy bear cholla, is a type of prickly pear cactus native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. It has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to treat wounds, burns, and skin irritations. The health benefits of Opuntia infesta include its ability to lower cholesterol levels, improve digestion, and reduce the risk of certain diseases such as diabetes and certain types of cancer. The plant can be used in herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and topical creams, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
